examples.title: Instruction Fetch-Decode-Execute Cycle
examples.description: Demonstrates the basic operation of CPU instruction processing
examples.code:
PROCEDURE ExecuteInstruction
BEGIN
  // FETCH PHASE
  instructionAddress <- ProgramCounter
  instruction <- ReadMemory(instructionAddress)
  ProgramCounter <- ProgramCounter + 1
  
  // DECODE PHASE
  operationCode <- ExtractOpCode(instruction)
  operands <- ExtractOperands(instruction)
  
  // EXECUTE PHASE
  SWITCH operationCode
    CASE ADD:
      result <- ALU.Add(operands.source1, operands.source2)
    CASE SUB:
      result <- ALU.Subtract(operands.source1, operands.source2)
    CASE LOAD:
      result <- ReadMemory(operands.address)
    CASE STORE:
      WriteMemory(operands.address, operands.value)
    CASE JUMP:
      ProgramCounter <- operands.address
  END SWITCH
  
  // STORE PHASE
  IF operation.requiresStore THEN
    WriteRegister(operation.destination, result)
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the four phases of instruction execution: FETCH gets the instruction from memory, DECODE interprets what operation to perform, EXECUTE performs the operation using ALU, and STORE saves the result back to registers or memory.

examples.title: Simple Bootstrap Loader Simulation
examples.description: Simulates loading operating system into memory during boot process
examples.code:
class BootstrapLoader:
    def __init__(self, memory_size):
        self.memory = [0] * memory_size
        self.os_load_address = 100  # OS loads at memory location 100
        self.boot_device = "hard_drive"
    
    def power_on_self_test(self):
        print("POST: Checking hardware components...")
        print("- Memory check: OK")
        print("- Keyboard: OK")
        print("- Display: OK")
        print("System ready.")
    
    def read_boot_sector(self):
        print(f"Reading boot sector from {self.boot_device}...")
        boot_code = [0xB8, 0x00, 0x00, 0x90, 0xCB]  # Sample machine code
        return boot_code
    
    def load_bootloader(self):
        print("Loading bootstrap loader from ROM...")
        return True
    
    def load_os(self):
        print("Locating operating system...")
        os_kernel = self.read_boot_sector()
        print(f"Loading OS kernel to memory address {self.os_load_address}...")
        for i, byte in enumerate(os_kernel):
            self.memory[self.os_load_address + i] = byte
        print("OS loaded successfully.")
    
    def boot(self):
        self.power_on_self_test()
        if self.load_bootloader():
            self.load_os()
            print("Boot complete. Operating system ready.")

# Simulation
boot_loader = BootstrapLoader(1024)
boot_loader.boot()
examples.explanation: This Python code simulates the boot process: POST checks hardware, bootstrap loader is loaded from ROM, then it reads the boot sector and loads the OS kernel into memory at a specific address.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is size of OS kernel
examples.spaceComplexity: O(m) where m is memory size

examples.title: DMA Transfer Simulation
examples.description: Demonstrates Direct Memory Access data transfer without CPU intervention
examples.explanation: This C code demonstrates DMA operation: The CPU sets up the DMA controller with source, destination, and transfer count, then starts the transfer. While DMA transfers data directly between I/O device and memory, the CPU is free to perform other tasks, improving overall system performance.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is transfer size
